rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference

Identify hub genes that recur across cliques

Source: R/cliques.R
clique_hubs.Rd

For each gene appearing in the output of find_cliques, counts how many cliques it participates in (clique degree). A HOG can produce multiple cliques with different species subsets; n_cliques counts total clique appearances, while n_hogs counts distinct HOGs. When stability results are provided, each clique appearance is weighted by its phylogenetic stability score.

clique_hubs(
  cliques,
  target_species,
  species_trait = NULL,
  stability = NULL,
  min_cliques = 2L
)

Arguments

cliques

Output of find_cliques.

target_species

Character vector of species (must match column names in cliques).

species_trait

Optional named character or factor vector mapping species to trait groups (same format as clique_stability). When provided, cliques are annotated by trait exclusivity and per-trait counts are included in the output.

stability

Optional output of clique_stability. When provided, adds stability-weighted columns: mean_stability (mean k=1 stability score across the gene's exclusive cliques) and n_stable (count of exclusive cliques with stability_score = 1.0 at k=1). Requires species_trait to also be provided.

min_cliques

Minimum number of clique appearances to include a gene in the output (default 2).

Value

A data frame sorted by n_stable (if stability provided), n_exclusive (if trait provided), or n_cliques, with:

gene

Gene identifier

species

Species the gene belongs to

n_cliques

Number of cliques this gene participates in

n_hogs

Number of distinct HOGs (secondary; equals n_cliques when each HOG produces one clique)

n_exclusive

Number of trait-exclusive clique appearances (only present when species_trait is provided)

n_

One column per trait level counting exclusive clique appearances (only present when species_trait is provided)

mean_stability

Mean stability score (k=1) across exclusive cliques (only present when stability is provided)

n_stable

Count of exclusive cliques with perfect stability at k=1 (only present when stability is provided)

Contents
